name: Date (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh (as-eaten) weight; ripe/tamr stage, Deglet Noor reference (USDA FDC 171726); water ~20.5 g/100 g

components.name: water
components.evidence: Moisture of tamr-stage Deglet Noor dates; ~25% at tamr stage across reviews (PMC11011438).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Dominant fermentable monosaccharide; dates are essentially invert-sugar (glucose+fructose)-rich fruit.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Co-dominant monosaccharide; ~equal to glucose in Deglet Noor.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Deglet Noor retains high sucrose (~38% of sugars); note Medjool has ~0 sucrose and glucose/fructose ~33/32 g (FDC 171707) — cultivar-dependent.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Minor disaccharide.

components.name: dietary fiber (incl. pectic substances)
components.evidence: Includes pectin/pectic acid and cellulose; largely non-fermentable by most single microbes but relevant matrix carbon.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: High in unripe (khalal) dates but nearly fully hydrolysed to sugars by the tamr stage; negligible/trace in ripe fruit. Present but unquantified for ripe Deglet Noor.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Al-Farsi & Lee reviews / Moroccan-syrup study (S0889157520302374); Barhee developmental study (RG 260390876)
components.evidence: Predominant organic acid in dates. Total organic acids in dates 116-1003 mg/100 g; malic is the largest single fraction. Midpoint estimate ~220 mg/100g FW (range wide, cultivar/maturity dependent).

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Reported as the MAIN acid at the tamr (fully ripe) stage as malic declines; estimate within the 116-1003 mg/100g total-acid envelope.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Second-ranked acid after malic in date syrups; midpoint estimate on fresh-fruit basis.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid in dates (dates are a low-acid, malic-dominant fruit, NOT citric-dominant).

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: 18.47-233.35 mg/100 g dry weight across cultivars; midpoint ~50-70 mg/100g FW.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: 115.7-484.2 mg/100 g DW reported in some Moroccan cultivars; present but inconsistent across studies, so left unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low but non-trivial N source; free amino acids (esp. glutamate, aspartate, proline) are elevated in dates.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in date protein.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: USDA aspartic acid pools free aspartate + asparagine.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Elevated free proline typical of dates.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Reported as cystine (2x cysteine); sulfur amino acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA lists L-serine (the current record's 'D-Serine' is a BiGG-mapping artifact).

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Missing from current record; added.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral in dates.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single iron pool; compartmentalised Fe2+/Fe3+ entries in the current record are BiGG artifacts (see remove[]).

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Ultratrace but genuine.

components.name: boron
components.evidence: Dates are a genuinely boron-rich fruit; present but not consistently quantified. Kept (unlike aluminium).

components.name: vitamin C (L-ascorbate)
components.evidence: Low; dates are not a vitamin C source.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Most abundant B-vitamin in dates.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: mcg DFE.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant food).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Pro-vitamin A precursor; retinol itself is 0.

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Dominant carotenoid fraction in dates.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Dates are essentially fat-free; fatty acids are trace.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1)
components.evidence: Main MUFA (as total monounsaturated).

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2)
components.evidence: Main PUFA (as total polyunsaturated).

components.name: total saturated fatty acids
components.evidence: Includes trace lauric/capric/palmitic (the current record's decanoic/dodecanoic/octanoic/heptanoic entries).

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: Non-nutritive toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Ultratrace cobalt; dates contain no B12, so no meaningful cobalt requirement; FooDB trace-element noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace non-nutritive element; not a defining or required date nutrient.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultratrace non-nutritive element; FooDB trace noise for a growth medium.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate/compartment representation of the single iron pool (BiGG artifact); collapsed into one 'iron'.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged BiGG artifact, not a distinct nutrient; collapsed into one 'iron'.

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: Non-nutritive contaminant element, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element for microbial growth; matrix contaminant.

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: Isoflavone characteristic of legumes/soy; biologically implausible in date fruit; FooDB predicted-not-measured noise.

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: Legume/soy phytoestrogen class; implausible in dates; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin from Apiaceae (parsnip/celery); implausible in date fruit; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Bacterial (Streptomyces) fermentation biopolymer; not a native date compound; FooDB artifact.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace flavour volatile aldehyde, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Predicted racemic/diastereomer artifact of isoleucine; duplicate of L-isoleucine.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Stereochemistry artifact; the biological amino acid in date protein is L-serine (added).

missing_added: malic acid (dominant organic acid, previously absent)
acetic acid, succinic acid, citric acid, oxalic acid (organic acids, previously absent)
individual sugars with real amounts (glucose 19.9, fructose 19.6, sucrose 23.8 g/100g)
L-phenylalanine, L-valine (amino acids missing from current record)
quantitative vitamin panel (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9, C, E, K, choline, betaine)
quantitative mineral panel (K, P, Mg, Ca, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
water content, total protein, dietary fiber/pectin
keep_uncovered: gallic acid, ferulic acid, caffeic acid, p-coumaric acid, o-coumaric acid, syringic acid (genuine date phenolic acids, no BiGG met)
chlorogenic/quinic-family acids, procyanidins / Chinese tannin (condensed tannins, genuine)
cyanidin (anthocyanidin, genuine in dark date cultivars)
boron (genuine date micronutrient, no BiGG exchange)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171726 (Dates, Deglet Noor) — full nutrient panel incl. individual sugars, amino acids, vitamins, minerals, fatty acids
USDA FoodData Central FDC 171707 (Dates, Medjool) — cultivar contrast for sugar profile
Moroccan date-syrup organic-acid & mineral study, J. Food Compos. Anal. doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2020.103576 (S0889157520302374)
Analysis of Sugars and Organic Acids of Date Palm 'Barhee' during fruit development (ResearchGate 260390876)
An Overview of Date Fruits as a Global Food Resource, PMC11011438 (moisture, macronutrient ranges)
notes: Dates are a high-sugar, low-acid fruit: the dominant carbon/energy source is invert sugar — near-equal glucose (~19.9) and fructose (~19.6 g/100g) plus abundant sucrose (~23.8 g/100g in Deglet Noor; note Medjool is sucrose-free with glucose/fructose ~33/32 g/100g). Organic acids are minor and malic-dominant (acetic rises at the fully ripe tamr stage); total organic acids ~0.1-1 g/100g. Values are for ripe Deglet Noor (~20% water); Medjool differs mainly in the sugar split.
